data.narrative:
Ocius Technology is a Series B–stage defense and security company founded by Robert Dane. The peer universe consists of 10 comparables: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms, including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

The peer-median method is applicable and forms the basis of valuation. Across six same-sector, same-stage private comparables, the median valuation is $1.2B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.5B. The sample includes valuations of $1.5B (Vannevar Labs, Dream Security), $1.4B (Harmattan AI), and $1.0B (Castelion, Defense Unicorns). This method alone supports an implied valuation of $1.2B.

The triangulated analysis yields a median of $1.2B, with a low estimate of $1.0B and a high estimate of $1.4B. The relatively tight clustering of peer valuations suggests limited downside dispersion in the Series B defense-tech cohort. Key uncertainties include execution risk on the A$176M Australian Program of Record contract awarded in March 2026—revenue realization and timing remain material variables—and limited public market comparables for direct calibration. The valuation range is defensible on peer-set grounds but carries material execution dependency.
